description: Maps VPE classifications to OMNIX connectors. Higher score = better fit. Editable — reload via /api/connectors/reload-registry.

connectors.hint: If this is a DJI Tello, use the Tello connector — it speaks the open Tello SDK over Wi-Fi UDP.
For PX4/ArduPilot drones (Pixhawk, Holybro, etc.) — works over serial/UDP/TCP.
DIY drone built on a Raspberry Pi — install the OMNIX Pi agent.
DIY drone built on an ESP32 — flash the OMNIX ESP32 sketch.
Raspberry Pi-based rover — use the Pi agent.
Arduino Uno/Nano/Mega driving the motors — connect via USB serial.
ESP32-based rover — flash the OMNIX sketch.
If your rover already runs ROS2 (Nav2 etc.), bridge topics directly.
Some rovers use ArduRover/MAVLink — try this if you have a Pixhawk.
Most modern arms (UR, Franka) have ROS2 drivers — bridge is the fastest route.
Hobby arm (MeArm, SO-100, Dobot clone) driven by Arduino — USB serial works great.
ESP32-based arm — flash the OMNIX sketch.
Pi driving servos via GPIO/PWM hat.
Industrial arms almost always have a ROS2 driver — use the bridge.
Only for DIY industrial setups.
Humanoids (Pepper, NAO, custom) typically expose ROS topics.
DIY humanoid with a Pi brain.
Most open quadrupeds (Unitree, MIT Mini Cheetah) have ROS2 support.
DIY quadruped with a Pi — install the agent.
ESP32-based quadruped — flash the OMNIX sketch.
DIY vacuum/mower brain on a Pi.
ESP32 brain — flash the OMNIX sketch.
Some iRobot / research platforms run ROS2.
Service robots (delivery, hospitality) usually run ROS2.
DIY builds on a Pi.
AMRs (Fetch, MiR, Otto) are ROS-native — bridge topics.
Only for DIY AMRs.
Medical robots (da Vinci research kit, etc.) expose ROS interfaces.
ESP32 with PWM/NeoPixel — flash the OMNIX sketch.
Pi-controlled LED setup.
Arduino + LED driver over USB.
ESP32 is the most common brain for smart devices — flash the sketch.
Arduino over USB serial.
Pi-hosted control.
Ardusub / BlueROV and most marine vehicles speak MAVLink.
DIY boats on a Pi.
Research space robots often run ROS2.
Some CubeSat dev kits reuse MAVLink.
Field / extreme-environment robots mostly run ROS2.
DIY rugged builds on a Pi.
